source: WhatsApp research showing physical phone requirement + ban risk
summary: Telegram Bot API as primary communication channel, WhatsApp deferred

community_evidence.production_deployments: Telegram Bot API is the standard for server-to-user notifications in the agent community
community_evidence.practitioner_sentiment: Zero friction, zero ban risk, designed for automation

evaluation.cost_of_staying: WhatsApp via Baileys needs physical phone + QR scan + ban risk. WhatsApp Business API needs Meta verification + per-message cost.

rationale: John prefers WhatsApp but three paths evaluated: Baileys (free but needs physical phone for QR scan, moderate ban risk), Business API (no phone but Meta verification takes days, $0.004-0.14/msg), Telegram (free, no phone, zero ban risk, 3-minute setup, designed for automation). Telegram wins on zero friction. WhatsApp deferred as future macro evolution decision. OpenClaw supports both as built-in channels.

test_results.notes: Exact OpenClaw Telegram config researched and documented in wiki/openclaw-setup.md. Known bug: first message must come from user before bot can send proactively.

rollback.instructions: Switch to WhatsApp by updating openclaw.json channels config
